preconditions: KOZO ABI V1 normative layer is in place
Odin and Rust toolchains are installed
assumptions: Protocol validation is semantic and repository-grounded rather than runtime-executed

steps.title: Align the ABI, kernel dispatcher, Rust consumer, and harness protocol validator

expected_outputs: The kernel dispatches ABI syscalls through generated constants
The Rust service uses ABI syscall identifiers in a typed request path
The harness verifies semantic protocol alignment across header, kernel, and service layers

invariants: All dispatch and request logic uses generated ABI identifiers
Protocol drift is detected semantically across contract, kernel, and service layers
expected_behavior: verify.sh fails when the heartbeat syscall is absent from the kernel dispatcher
verify.sh passes when header, kernel, and service remain aligned
actual_behavior: verify.sh reports protocol_alignment in the final artifact.
The protocol validator fails with PROTOCOL_MISMATCH when the heartbeat syscall case is removed from the kernel dispatcher.
verify.sh passes once the kernel dispatcher, Rust consumer, and normative ABI are aligned.

explanation_summary: Added an ABI-backed syscall dispatch path in the Odin kernel using generated ABI identifiers only.
Updated the Rust core service to use ABI syscall identifiers through a typed heartbeat_request path.
Added semantic protocol validation so the harness detects missing kernel dispatch cases and missing Rust syscall usage.
